skill.name: git-submodule
skill.description: Manage Git submodules for including external repositories within a main repository. Use when working with external libraries, shared modules, or managing dependencies as separate Git repositories.
skill.summary: Manage Git submodules for including external repositories within a main repository.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation skill containing Git submodule commands and examples. Static analysis flagged bash commands in markdown code blocks and Git URLs as security concerns, but these are all false positives. The skill contains no executable code, only documentation explaining Git submodule operations. All 82 detected patterns were evaluated: external_commands are markdown code fences, network patterns are example URLs in documentation, and filesystem patterns are standard shell navigation commands. No malicious intent or actual security risks found.

content.user_title: Manage Git Submodules in Your Projects
content.value_statement: Managing external Git repositories as submodules can be complex. This skill provides step-by-step commands and examples for adding, updating, cloning, and removing Git submodules in your projects.
actual_capabilities: Add external Git repositories as submodules to a project
Clone repositories with submodules using recursive initialization
Update submodules to latest remote versions or specific commits
Work inside submodules and push changes to their repositories
Run batch operations across all submodules in a project
Remove submodules completely including cleanup of Git cache
limitations: Submodules reference specific commits, not branches, requiring manual updates
Private submodules require proper SSH key or token configuration
Nested submodules can complicate workflows and increase complexity
Converting between submodules and regular directories requires manual steps

use_cases.title: Include External Libraries in Your Project
use_cases.description: Add third-party libraries or frameworks as Git submodules to include them directly in your repository while keeping them as separate versioned projects.

use_cases.title: Share Code Across Multiple Projects
use_cases.description: Maintain a shared component library used by multiple repositories. Update the submodule in one place and pull changes across all projects that use it.

use_cases.title: Set Up CI/CD with Submodules
use_cases.description: Configure GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or Jenkins to properly clone and initialize submodules during automated build and deployment pipelines.

prompt_templates.title: Add a submodule to my project
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a Git submodule from https://github.com/example/lib.git to the libs/lib path in my project
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to include an external library as a submodule

prompt_templates.title: Clone repository with submodules
prompt_templates.prompt: Clone the repository at [URL] and initialize all submodules recursively
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to clone a project that uses submodules

prompt_templates.title: Update all submodules to latest
prompt_templates.prompt: Update all submodules in this project to their latest remote versions and show me what changed
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to update all dependencies at once

prompt_templates.title: Remove a submodule completely
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the submodule at libs/lib from this project including all Git cache and configuration
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to cleanly remove a submodule

output_examples.input: Add a submodule from https://github.com/lodash/lodash.git to vendor/lodash
output_examples.output: Added submodule for lodash library. The library is now tracked at vendor/lodash and locked to the current commit. You can update to newer versions using 'git submodule update --remote vendor/lodash'.

output_examples.input: Clone myproject repository and initialize all submodules
output_examples.output: Cloned myproject successfully. Initialized and updated all submodules recursively. Use 'git submodule status' to verify all submodules are at their expected commits.

best_practices: Lock submodules to specific commits or tags rather than branches for reproducible builds
Always initialize and update submodules with --recursive when working with nested dependencies
Document submodule setup instructions in your project README for other developers
anti_patterns: Do not use submodules for frequently changing dependencies that need automatic updates
Avoid deeply nested submodule chains as they complicate maintenance and debugging
Do not forget to update the parent repository reference after making changes in a submodule

faq.question: What is a Git submodule?
faq.answer: A Git submodule is a reference to a specific commit of an external Git repository embedded within your main repository. It allows you to include and track external code while keeping it as a separate versioned project.

faq.question: How do I clone a repository with submodules?
faq.answer: Use git clone with --recursive: git clone --recursive <repo-url>. Or clone first then run: git submodule update --init --recursive

faq.question: How do I update a submodule to the latest version?
faq.answer: Run git submodule update --remote <path> to fetch the latest from the default branch, or git submodule update --remote --merge to fetch and merge changes automatically.

faq.question: How do I completely remove a submodule?
faq.answer: Run git submodule deinit <path>, then git rm <path>, then rm -rf .git/modules/<path>. Finally commit the changes.

faq.question: Why is my submodule showing as detached HEAD?
faq.answer: Submodules checkout specific commits by default, not branches. Checkout a branch with git checkout main inside the submodule directory to start making changes.

faq.question: Can submodules work with private repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, but you need proper authentication. Use SSH URLs (git@github.com:org/repo.git) with SSH keys configured, or use HTTPS with credentials stored securely.
